reasoning: Copernicus (ERA5) reports October 2025 was +0.70°C above the 1991-2020 baseline, making it the 3rd warmest October on record. By comparison, October 2023 was +0.85°C in ERA5 and mapped to +1.34°C in GISTEMP (1951-1980 baseline), implying an offset of ~0.49°C between the two metrics for this time of year. Applying that offset to October 2025's +0.70°C gives an expected GISTEMP anomaly of roughly +1.19°C. An outcome below 1.10°C would require the GISTEMP anomaly to be at least 0.09°C cooler than expected, which is roughly two standard deviations away given the high correlation between these datasets. Thus, it is highly unlikely to resolve YES.
